description: Persist the AWN Gateway world registry across restarts, clean up misleading registry naming, harden lifecycle shutdown behavior, and improve health-check observability without changing public endpoint behavior.

userStories.title: Rename gateway peer terminology to registry terminology
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Gateway's internal naming to reflect its role as a world registry so that the code is self-documenting without changing runtime behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: `const peers = new Map()` is renamed to `const registry = new Map()`
`upsertPeer()` is renamed to `upsertAgent()`
`pruneStale()` is renamed to `pruneStaleAgents()`
`getPeersForExchange()` is renamed to `getAgentsForExchange()`
`MAX_PEERS` is renamed to `MAX_AGENTS`
All references to the old names in `gateway/server.mjs` are updated
`/health` response field `peers` is renamed to `agents`
All existing endpoints return the same data as before except for the `/health` field rename
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add registry load and atomic save helpers
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Gateway to load and save registry data in `$DATA_DIR/registry.json` so that restart recovery does not depend on fresh announces.
acceptanceCriteria: `loadRegistry()` reads `$DATA_DIR/registry.json` during startup
`loadRegistry()` discards entries older than `STALE_TTL_MS` before adding them to the in-memory registry
`saveRegistry()` writes registry state using a 1-second debounce
`flushRegistry()` writes registry state immediately
Registry writes are atomic by writing `registry.json.tmp` and then renaming it to `registry.json`
The persisted file schema is `{ "version": 1, "savedAt": <ms>, "agents": { <agentId>: <record> } }`
If `registry.json` is missing or invalid on startup, the server logs a warning and starts with an empty registry
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire persistence into announce and prune flows
userStories.description: As an operator, I want registry mutations to persist automatically so that normal announce and prune activity keeps the on-disk registry current.
acceptanceCriteria: `upsertAgent()` schedules a debounced `saveRegistry()` when announce data changes the registry
`pruneStaleAgents()` calls `flushRegistry()` immediately when at least one stale entry is removed
Startup loads persisted registry entries into the in-memory registry before serving requests
No new files or dependencies are added outside `gateway/server.mjs`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Stop `/peer/message` from polluting the registry and tune stale cleanup timing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `/peer/message` to avoid creating empty registry records and stale cleanup to run faster so that the registry stays accurate and dead worlds disappear sooner.
acceptanceCriteria: The `/peer/message` handler no longer calls `upsertAgent()` with an empty address record
The `/peer/message` handler still relays `world.state` broadcasts to WebSocket subscribers
`STALE_TTL_MS` default is changed from `30 * 60 * 1000` to `15 * 60 * 1000`
The prune interval is changed from `5 * 60 * 1000` to `3 * 60 * 1000`
All existing peer protocol endpoints continue to work identically except that `/peer/message` no longer upserts registry entries
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add graceful shutdown that flushes registry state
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the Gateway to flush registry data and close cleanly on process signals so that shutdown does not lose recent updates or leave background work running.
acceptanceCriteria: `startPeerListener()` returns the Fastify instance it creates
`SIGTERM` handler calls `flushRegistry()`, closes both Fastify servers, and clears active intervals
`SIGINT` handler calls `flushRegistry()`, closes both Fastify servers, and clears active intervals
Shutdown logic runs without introducing new dependencies or files
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Enhance `/health` with registry age and readiness status
userStories.description: As an operator, I want `/health` to report registry freshness and readiness state so that deployment monitoring can distinguish empty, warming, and ready gateway states.
acceptanceCriteria: `/health` includes `registryAge` as milliseconds since the last registry modification, or `null` when the registry is empty
`/health` includes `status: "ready"` when worlds count is greater than 0
`/health` includes `status: "warming"` when agents count is greater than 0 and worlds count is 0
`/health` includes `status: "empty"` when agents count is 0
`/health` keeps `ok: true` for backward-compatible health checks
Existing `/health` consumers can continue reading the endpoint without authentication or path changes
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
